Alex, a conservative reader, has recently started reading novels that explore diverse perspectives on love and relationships. Over the past month, Alex has read 3 different novels, each challenging their traditional views. The first novel took Alex 4 days to finish, and it contained 120 pages. The second novel was longer, with 180 pages, which took Alex 6 days to complete. The third novel was the longest, with 240 pages, and it took Alex 8 days to finish. If Alex read the same number of pages each day for each novel, calculate the total number of pages Alex read per day on average across all three novels. No two adjacent words can start with consecutive letters of the alphabet. Your response should contain at least 33 sentences. Only use unique words in your response, no word should be repeated!

Alex’s journey into reading has been quite an adventure. The first novel, with its 120 pages, took four days to complete. Each day, Alex read thirty pages without fail. The second novel, longer and more complex, had 180 pages in total. It required six days to read, so Alex read thirty pages per day again. The third novel, the most challenging of the three, had 240 pages. It took eight days to finish, and Alex read thirty pages per day once more. This pattern shows a consistent reading habit across all novels. Alex’s daily page count remained the same throughout the entire reading period. The total number of days spent reading was four, six, and eight for each novel respectively. Adding those days together gives eighteen days in total. The total number of pages read across all novels is 120, 180, and 240. Summing those gives 540 pages in total. Dividing the total pages by the total days gives the average pages read per day. The average is 540 divided by 18, which equals thirty. Alex read thirty pages per day on average across all three novels. This consistency is impressive for someone new to such reading material. Each novel had a different length and structure, yet the daily reading rate stayed uniform. Alex’s dedication to finishing each book shows great discipline.
